visa_free.name: China (mainland, up to 30 days; HK SAR up to 14 days; Macao SAR up to 90 days)
Dominican Republic (tourist pass obtainable at airport, $10)

visa_on_arrival.name: Egypt (at tourist airports: Hurghada, Marsa Alam, Sharm El-Sheikh; Cairo requires prior visa)
Maldives (free tourist visa at airport, up to 30 days)
Mauritius (at border for tourist/business/transit up to 60 days)
Palau (30-day tourist visa on arrival, extendable)
Rwanda (single-entry visa on arrival or online)
Thailand (visa on arrival, valid 15 days, from Jul 2024)

eta.name: Israel (ETA-IL required before arrival, ~EUR 6.75, 2 years validity; MFA advises against travel due to security)
Kenya (eTA required, apply at etakenya.go.ke, at least 3 days before travel)
South Korea (K-ETA required, ~EUR 7.50, 3 years validity; exemptions for under-17 and over-65)

outbound_official.notes: Data sourced from Serbian MFA per-country pages at mfa.gov.rs/en/citizens/travel-abroad/visas-and-states-travel-advisory/<country>. The MFA does not publish a single consolidated visa-free list; each country page must be consulted individually. All entries above were individually confirmed from official MFA pages. Some destinations listed here require ordinary (biometric) passport — non-biometric passport holders may face different rules. Jamaica listed as visa-required by MFA for ordinary passport but de-facto visa-free for holders of valid US/Canada/UK/Schengen visas. Mexico listed as visa-required but free-of-charge; exemption for holders of valid US/Canada/Japan/UK/Schengen visas/residence permits. Dominican Republic tourist pass ($10) obtainable at airport counters is treated as visa-free by MFA. Latvia introduced ETA requirement from September 1, 2025 (eta.gov.lv). Not all 194 listed countries on the MFA index were fetched — approximately 130 individual pages were reviewed; remaining countries (notably many African, Pacific Island, and some Asian nations) are likely visa-required but were not individually verified.

name: Serbia
Serbia

visa_free.notes: Citizens of all EU member states may enter visa-free. 90 days within a 6-month period. ID card accepted as travel document (confirmed individually for several EU members).
No visas required for visits of up to 90 days in a 6 months period starting from the date of the first entry. ID card accepted as an alternative travel document.
No visas required for visits of up to 90 days in a 6 months period starting from the date of the first entry. ID card accepted as an alternative travel document.
No visas required for visits of up to 90 days. ID card accepted as an alternative travel document.
No visas required for visits of up to 90 days in a 6 months period starting from the date of the first entry. ID card accepted as an alternative travel document.
No visas required for visits of up to 90 days in a 6 months period starting from the date of the first entry (ordinary passports).
No visas required for visits of up to 90 days in a 6 months period starting from the date of the first entry (ordinary passports).
No visas required for visits of up to 90 days in a 6 months period starting from the date of the first entry (ordinary passports).
No visas required for visits of up to 90 days in a 6 months period starting from the date of the first entry (ordinary passports).
No visas required for visits of up to 90 days in the period of 6 months from the date of first entry (ordinary passports).
No visas required for visits of up to 90 days in 6 months period from first entrance (ordinary passports).
Ordinary passport holders: no visas required for visits of up to 30 days. Diplomatic and official passport holders: no visas required for visits of up to 90 days.

Ordinary passport holders: no visas required for visits of up to 30 days. Diplomatic, official, and business passport holders: no visas required. Hong Kong SAR passport holders: no visas required for visits up to 14 days. Macau SAR passport holders: no visas required for visits up to 90 days.

e_visa.nationality: 46 nationalities (complete list not publicly enumerated on official portal)
e_visa.notes: Serbia launched Digital Travel Authorizations (electronic Visa C) on 23 April 2025 for citizens of 46 countries that require a visa to enter Serbia. First e-visa arrivals at Nikola Tesla Airport on 9 June 2025. Visa C valid for up to 90 days, single or multiple entries within 180 days. Primarily for citizens of countries without Serbian diplomatic or consular missions, particularly in South America, Africa, and Asia. Lesotho was specifically named as one eligible country. Processing as fast as 1 hour when complete documentation submitted. Applications submitted 24/7 at evisa.welcometoserbia.gov.rs.

visa_policy.notes: The official MFA visa regime page (mfa.gov.rs/en/citizens/travel-serbia/visa-regime) lists approximately 195 countries/territories, each with individual pages. The index page does not group countries by visa-free/visa-required status; individual country pages must be consulted. The visa_free list above reflects only countries individually confirmed from official MFA country pages during this validation. A comprehensive visa-free country list cannot be fully verified without fetching all ~195 individual pages. The previously recorded 'EEA / Schengen Area countries' bloc entry has been removed as it duplicated the EU Member States entry and introduced ambiguity — specific EEA/non-EU/non-Schengen countries (Norway, Iceland, Liechtenstein, Switzerland) have not been individually confirmed and remain in gaps.

conditional_access.conditions: Foreign nationals holding all types of national passports with a valid Schengen visa may enter, transit and stay in the Republic of Serbia up to 90 days during a six-month period, and within validity of said visas. Two-passport rule applies: may present expired national passport with valid visa sticker together with a valid national passport without visa. Residence permit in card form must be accompanied by valid passport.

Foreign nationals holding all types of national passports with a valid UK visa may enter, transit and stay in the Republic of Serbia up to 90 days during a six-month period, and within validity of said visas.

credential.subtype: any valid EU Member State visa

conditional_access.conditions: Foreign nationals holding all types of national passports with a valid EU Member States' visa may enter, transit and stay in the Republic of Serbia up to 90 days during a six-month period, and within validity of said visas.

Foreign nationals holding all types of national passports with a valid US visa may enter, transit and stay in the Republic of Serbia up to 90 days during a six-month period, and within validity of said visas.

credential.subtype: any valid Schengen area residence permit

conditional_access.conditions: Foreign nationals holding a residence permit in the countries of the Schengen area may enter, transit and stay in the Republic of Serbia up to 90 days during a six-month period, and within validity of said permit. Residence permit in card form must be accompanied by valid passport.

Foreign nationals holding a residence permit in the EU may enter, transit and stay in the Republic of Serbia up to 90 days during a six-month period, and within validity of said permit. Residence permit in card form must be accompanied by valid passport.

Foreign nationals holding a residence permit in the United States may enter, transit and stay in the Republic of Serbia up to 90 days during a six-month period, and within validity of said permit. Residence permit in card form must be accompanied by valid passport.

cbi.notes: Serbia does not have a formal Citizenship-by-Investment program. The MFA citizenship page (mfa.gov.rs/en/citizens/services/citizenship) lists four standard citizenship pathways (by origin, by admission with 3 years permanent residence, by admission via spouse, by admission for diaspora) with no investment-based citizenship route. A separate 'Citizenship by Exception' pathway exists under Article 19 of the Law on Citizenship (government discretion for national interest), but this is not a structured CBI program with defined investment thresholds — it is a discretionary grant. No official government source enumerates a CBI program with set amounts or procedures.

programs.name: Temporary Residence for Investors
programs.notes: Requires certificate or proof of funds of at least EUR 50,000 in a Serbian-registered bank account and proof of invested tangible and intangible assets in a registered company in the Republic of Serbia (confirmed on official welcometoserbia.gov.rs page). For permit extension, proof of paid cash capital contribution of at least EUR 5,000 into share capital of a registered Serbian company is required. Permit issued for up to 3 years, renewable. Path to permanent residence requires 3 years of continuous temporary residence; citizenship eligibility after 3 further years of permanent residence (total ~6 years). Based on Law on Foreigners (amended 2023, in force February 2024).

programs.name: Temporary Residence for Startup Founders
programs.notes: Available to startup founders with innovative business activities confirmed by a Serbian science and technology park. Permit issued for up to 3 years under the Law on Foreigners (2023 amendments). Specific eligibility criteria and financial thresholds not publicly detailed on official portal pages accessed.

programs.name: Temporary Residence for Talent
programs.notes: Available to holders of tertiary education qualifications that are professionally recognized (nostrified diploma required). Permit issued for up to 3 years under the Law on Foreigners (2023 amendments). Specific financial thresholds not detailed on official portal pages accessed.

programs.name: Temporary Residence for Real Estate Owners
programs.notes: Residence permit available on the basis of real estate ownership in Serbia. No government-mandated minimum investment threshold stated on official sources. Permit issued for up to 3 years. Path to permanent residence requires 3 years continuous temporary residence.

programs.name: Digital Nomads / Remote Workers Temporary Residence
programs.eligibility: Foreign nationals working remotely for foreign employers or as self-employed individuals; must demonstrate proof of employment/contracts with foreign companies and sufficient funds
programs.processing_time: Average 19 working days (as reported post-2024 reform)
programs.notes: Serbia introduced tax incentives for digital nomads and a pathway via temporary residence permit (self-employment or employment basis). Not a dedicated branded 'digital nomad visa' — uses standard Visa D or temporary residence framework under the 2023 Law on Foreigners. Official welcome portal describes Serbia as committed to attracting digital nomads but does not publish a specific income threshold on the main digital nomad page.

programs.name: Single Temporary Residence and Work Permit
programs.eligibility: Foreign nationals employed in Serbia under employment or other contracts; intra-company transferees, independent professionals, trainees, self-employed
programs.notes: Unified single permit covering both residence and work rights. Issued for up to 36 months. Applications submitted exclusively electronically via welcometoserbia.gov.rs portal. Introduced under 2023 Law on Foreigners amendments effective February 2024.

agreements: Western Balkans Common Regional Market (freedom of movement with ID cards — signed November 2022; ratified by Albania, Montenegro, North Macedonia, Serbia; Bosnia and Herzegovina has not ratified)
CEFTA (Central European Free Trade Agreement) — member since December 2006 (parties: Albania, Bosnia and Herzegovina, Kosovo, Moldova, Montenegro, North Macedonia, Serbia)
Stabilisation and Association Agreement with EU (signed 2013)

gaps: The official MFA visa regime page lists approximately 195 countries individually but the index page does not group them by category. Only 13 individual country pages were fetched from official sources in this validation. A comprehensive visa-free country list cannot be verified country-by-country without fetching ~195 individual pages.

The complete list of 46 nationalities eligible for Serbia's e-visa (Digital Travel Authorization, launched April 23 2025) is referenced officially by the MFA but is not publicly enumerated on any accessible official page — the evisa.welcometoserbia.gov.rs portal requires login to see available nationalities.

Non-EU EEA countries (Norway, Iceland, Liechtenstein) and Switzerland — visa status not individually confirmed from official MFA pages in this validation round. Likely visa-free given Schengen membership but NOT confirmed from an official MFA per-country page.

Hong Kong SAR (ordinary BNO/HKSAR passport) and Macau SAR specific stay limits noted in China entry (14 days and 90 days respectively) but not recorded as separate entries — would need separate iso3 codes.

Specific minimum investment amounts, processing times, and eligibility criteria for the Startup and Talent temporary residence categories are not publicly detailed on the official welcometoserbia.gov.rs portal pages accessed.

Serbia's 'Citizenship by Exception' (Article 19, Law on Citizenship) is a discretionary government grant and not a structured CBI program; official details (criteria, fees, process) are not published on official government English pages.
No official government page in English enumerates the digital nomad income threshold — the EUR 3,500/month figure cited by some law firm sources could not be verified on official government pages.
Freedom of movement agreements under Western Balkans Common Regional Market are partially ratified — Bosnia and Herzegovina has not ratified the ID card free movement agreement as of 2024.

Diplomatic/service passport bilateral visa exemption agreements: 12 new agreements (Sao Tome and Principe, Togo, Maldives, Burundi, Guinea-Bissau, Uzbekistan, Ghana, Nepal, Republic of the Congo, Tajikistan, Eswatini, Saint Lucia) were presented to Serbian National Assembly; ratification status and entry-into-force dates unconfirmed. A comprehensive official list of ALL countries with existing bilateral diplomatic/service passport waiver agreements is not published in a single accessible English page on mfa.gov.rs.
No official Serbian government source found confirming OCI (Overseas Citizen of India) cardholder-specific entry rules.

The conditional_access rules for US/Schengen/EU/UK visa and residence permit holders do not specify whether single-use visas already used are valid. The official text states 'valid visa' without specifying prior-use restrictions.
